# Syvora

> Product studio: strategy, design, and engineering. 64 engineers across Blockchain & Web3, Full Stack Engineering, ServiceNow, and Extended Capabilities.

## Practices
- [Blockchain & Web3](https://syvora.com/practices/blockchain-web3/): Protocol, contracts, DeFi, tokenization, wallets, and security across the full on-chain stack.
- [Full Stack Engineering](https://syvora.com/practices/full-stack-engineering/): Web applications, APIs, cloud infrastructure, commerce, and the product layer around every system we ship.
- [ServiceNow](https://syvora.com/practices/servicenow/): ITSM, ITOM, HRSD, custom applications, integrations, and SLA-backed managed services on the Now Platform.
- [Extended Capabilities](https://syvora.com/practices/extended-capabilities/): AI automation, mobile, data, platform engineering, QA, and growth that plug into any engagement.

## Services
- [Smart Contract Development](https://syvora.com/services/smart-contract-development/): Solidity, Rust, and Move systems designed, tested, and hardened before mainnet.
- [Protocol & Token Design](https://syvora.com/services/protocol-token-design/): Tokenomics, value flow mapping, and incentive modeling before a single contract is written.
- [DeFi Development](https://syvora.com/services/defi-development/): AMMs, lending markets, staking, vaults, and derivatives infrastructure built to hold value.
- [Tokenization & RWA](https://syvora.com/services/tokenization-rwa/): Asset tokenization rails with compliance-aware architecture from day one.
- [Cross-Chain & Interoperability](https://syvora.com/services/cross-chain-interoperability/): Bridges, messaging layers, and coordinated multichain deployments.
- [Wallets & Account Abstraction](https://syvora.com/services/wallet-account-abstraction/): Embedded wallets, gasless flows, session keys, and onboarding UX that converts.
- [Security & Audit Readiness](https://syvora.com/services/smart-contract-audit-readiness/): Internal reviews, fuzzing, invariant testing, and external audit management.
- [On-Chain Data & Indexing](https://syvora.com/services/blockchain-indexing-data/): Subgraphs, custom indexers, analytics pipelines, and explorers.
- [Web Application Development](https://syvora.com/services/web-application-development/): React, Next.js, and TypeScript products taken from MVP to scale.
- [API & Backend Development](https://syvora.com/services/api-backend-development/): Node.js, Python, and Go services with REST, GraphQL, and event-driven architecture.
- [Cloud & Infrastructure](https://syvora.com/services/cloud-infrastructure/): AWS, GCP, and Cloudflare with IaC, CI/CD, and observability built in.
- [Database & Data Layer](https://syvora.com/services/database-architecture/): PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, and warehouse pipelines designed for growth.
- [Product Design & UI Engineering](https://syvora.com/services/product-design-ui-engineering/): Design systems, component libraries, accessibility, and motion.
- [Shopify & Commerce Engineering](https://syvora.com/services/shopify-commerce-engineering/): Shopify apps, storefronts, and checkout extensions running in production.
- [Performance & Reliability](https://syvora.com/services/performance-reliability-engineering/): Profiling, load testing, and SLO-driven operations.
- [Legacy Modernization](https://syvora.com/services/legacy-modernization/): Refactors and replatforms delivered without stopping the business.
- [ServiceNow ITSM Implementation](https://syvora.com/services/servicenow-itsm-implementation/): Incident, Problem, Change, Request, and Knowledge with ITIL-aligned process design.
- [ServiceNow ITOM & CMDB](https://syvora.com/services/servicenow-itom-cmdb/): Discovery, Service Mapping, Event Management, and CMDB health governance.
- [ServiceNow HRSD & CSM](https://syvora.com/services/servicenow-hrsd-csm/): Employee and customer workflows delivered through portal-first experiences.
- [ServiceNow Custom App Development](https://syvora.com/services/servicenow-custom-app-development/): Scoped applications on App Engine with Flow Designer and UI Builder.
- [ServiceNow Integrations](https://syvora.com/services/servicenow-integrations/): Integration Hub, REST and SOAP, MID Server, and bidirectional sync with enterprise systems.
- [Service Portal & Employee Center](https://syvora.com/services/servicenow-service-portal/): Adoption-focused experiences on top of the platform.
- [ServiceNow Virtual Agent & AI](https://syvora.com/services/servicenow-virtual-agent-ai/): Virtual Agent, Predictive Intelligence, and Now Assist enablement.
- [ServiceNow Managed Services](https://syvora.com/services/servicenow-managed-services/): Instance health scans, upgrade cycles, and ongoing SLA-backed support.
- [AI & Agentic Automation](https://syvora.com/services/ai-agentic-automation/): LLM integration, RAG pipelines, and agent workflows for real business operations.
- [Mobile App Development](https://syvora.com/services/mobile-app-development/): React Native and Flutter applications shipped to both app stores.
- [Data Engineering & Analytics](https://syvora.com/services/data-engineering-analytics/): Pipelines, warehouses, and dashboards that make product and on-chain data usable.
- [DevOps & Platform Engineering](https://syvora.com/services/devops-platform-engineering/): Kubernetes, Terraform, GitHub Actions, and full environment automation.
- [QA & Test Automation](https://syvora.com/services/qa-test-automation/): Playwright, Cypress, and API test suites wired directly into CI.
- [Technical SEO & Growth](https://syvora.com/services/technical-seo-growth/): Structured data, performance, and content architecture for organic acquisition.

## Guides & Comparisons
- [Smart Contract Audit Cost](https://syvora.com/guides/smart-contract-audit-cost/): indicative range $15K to $120K+
- [MVP Development Cost](https://syvora.com/guides/mvp-development-cost/): indicative range $40K to $150K
- [Shopify App Development Cost](https://syvora.com/guides/shopify-app-development-cost/): indicative range $15K to $60K+
- [ServiceNow Implementation Cost](https://syvora.com/guides/servicenow-implementation-cost/): indicative range $60K to $250K+
- [Mobile App Development Cost](https://syvora.com/guides/mobile-app-development-cost/): indicative range $50K to $200K+
- [DeFi Protocol Development Cost](https://syvora.com/guides/defi-protocol-development-cost/): indicative range $80K to $400K+
- [Token Launch Cost](https://syvora.com/guides/token-launch-cost/): indicative range $60K to $300K+
- [Web Application Development Cost](https://syvora.com/guides/web-application-development-cost/): indicative range $40K to $180K+
- [React Native vs Flutter](https://syvora.com/compare/react-native-vs-flutter/)
- [Solidity vs Rust](https://syvora.com/compare/solidity-vs-rust/)
- [Ethereum vs Solana](https://syvora.com/compare/ethereum-vs-solana/)
- [Base vs Arbitrum](https://syvora.com/compare/base-vs-arbitrum/)
- [AWS vs Google Cloud](https://syvora.com/compare/aws-vs-gcp/)
- [AWS vs Cloudflare](https://syvora.com/compare/aws-vs-cloudflare/)
- [PostgreSQL vs MongoDB](https://syvora.com/compare/postgresql-vs-mongodb/)
- [Node.js vs Go](https://syvora.com/compare/nodejs-vs-go/)
- [Kubernetes vs Serverless](https://syvora.com/compare/kubernetes-vs-serverless/)
- [Playwright vs Cypress](https://syvora.com/compare/playwright-vs-cypress/)
- [n8n vs Zapier](https://syvora.com/compare/n8n-vs-zapier/)
- [TypeScript vs JavaScript](https://syvora.com/compare/typescript-vs-javascript/)

## Glossary
- [Account Abstraction](https://syvora.com/glossary/account-abstraction/): A model where blockchain accounts are smart contracts instead of raw key pairs, enabling features like sponsored gas, session keys, and recovery without seed phrases.
- [ERC-4337](https://syvora.com/glossary/erc-4337/): The Ethereum standard that enables smart contract accounts without protocol changes, using a separate mempool of UserOperations processed by bundlers.
- [AMM (Automated Market Maker)](https://syvora.com/glossary/amm/): A decentralized exchange mechanism where liquidity pools and a pricing formula replace order books, letting anyone trade against pooled assets.
- [TVL (Total Value Locked)](https://syvora.com/glossary/tvl/): The total value of assets deposited in a protocol's contracts, used as the standard measure of DeFi protocol scale.
- [RWA Tokenization](https://syvora.com/glossary/rwa-tokenization/): Representing real world assets such as funds, credit, or property as on-chain tokens with legally enforced transfer rules.
- [ZK Rollup](https://syvora.com/glossary/zk-rollup/): A layer 2 that executes transactions off-chain and posts validity proofs to the base chain, inheriting its security with far lower costs.
- [Optimistic Rollup](https://syvora.com/glossary/optimistic-rollup/): A layer 2 that posts transactions to the base chain and assumes validity unless challenged during a dispute window.
- [MEV (Maximal Extractable Value)](https://syvora.com/glossary/mev/): Value extracted by reordering, inserting, or censoring transactions within a block, affecting protocols through sandwiching and liquidation races.
- [Oracle](https://syvora.com/glossary/oracle/): Infrastructure that brings off-chain data such as prices on-chain for contracts to consume.
- [Subgraph](https://syvora.com/glossary/subgraph/): An indexing definition on The Graph that transforms raw chain events into queryable GraphQL entities.
- [Chain Reorganization](https://syvora.com/glossary/chain-reorg/): When a blockchain replaces recent blocks with a competing branch, invalidating transactions applications may have already processed.
- [Multisig](https://syvora.com/glossary/multisig/): A wallet requiring multiple signatures to execute, standard for treasuries and protocol administration.
- [Timelock](https://syvora.com/glossary/timelock/): A contract that delays administrative actions by a fixed period after they are queued, giving users time to react to changes.
- [Gas Sponsorship](https://syvora.com/glossary/gas-sponsorship/): Paying transaction fees on behalf of users through paymasters, removing the largest onboarding objection in consumer crypto.
- [Invariant Testing](https://syvora.com/glossary/invariant-testing/): Property-based testing that asserts conditions which must always hold, such as conservation of funds, across randomized sequences of operations.
- [Fuzzing](https://syvora.com/glossary/fuzzing/): Automatically generating large volumes of randomized inputs to discover edge cases and crashes humans would not think to write.
- [Session Keys](https://syvora.com/glossary/session-keys/): Scoped, temporary signing keys that let a user approve a category of actions once instead of confirming every transaction.
- [CMDB (Configuration Management Database)](https://syvora.com/glossary/cmdb/): The system of record for IT assets and their relationships, underpinning impact analysis, change risk, and incident triage.
- [ITSM (IT Service Management)](https://syvora.com/glossary/itsm/): The discipline of running IT as defined services: incidents, problems, changes, requests, and knowledge.
- [ITOM (IT Operations Management)](https://syvora.com/glossary/itom/): The operational layer above the CMDB: discovery, service mapping, and event management that keep infrastructure visible and alert noise actionable.
- [HRSD (HR Service Delivery)](https://syvora.com/glossary/hrsd/): ServiceNow's HR case, knowledge, and lifecycle-event platform, giving employees one front door for HR while keeping sensitive cases confidential.
- [MID Server](https://syvora.com/glossary/mid-server/): A ServiceNow agent installed inside your network that lets the platform reach systems behind the firewall for discovery and integrations.
- [Flow Designer](https://syvora.com/glossary/flow-designer/): ServiceNow's low-code automation builder for approvals, tasks, and integrations without scripting.
- [Now Assist](https://syvora.com/glossary/now-assist/): ServiceNow's generative AI layer: case summarization, resolution notes, and agent assistance across workflows.
- [Service Catalog](https://syvora.com/glossary/service-catalog/): The storefront of requestable IT and business services, backed by fulfillment automation.
- [ATF (Automated Test Framework)](https://syvora.com/glossary/atf/): ServiceNow's native test automation, used to protect configurations and custom apps through upgrades.
- [RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)](https://syvora.com/glossary/rag/): An AI architecture where a model retrieves relevant documents before answering, grounding output in your actual data instead of training memory.
- [Agentic Workflow](https://syvora.com/glossary/agentic-workflow/): An automation where an AI model plans and executes multi-step tasks using tools, with permissions and human approval gates on consequential actions.
- [LLM Evaluation](https://syvora.com/glossary/llm-evaluation/): Testing AI outputs against golden datasets and scoring criteria so model or prompt changes cannot silently degrade quality.
- [Human-in-the-Loop](https://syvora.com/glossary/human-in-the-loop/): An automation pattern where defined steps pause for human review before execution, typically for consequential or ambiguous actions.
- [Strangler Pattern](https://syvora.com/glossary/strangler-pattern/): A modernization strategy where new capabilities are built beside a legacy system and traffic shifts gradually until the old system can be retired.
- [Core Web Vitals](https://syvora.com/glossary/core-web-vitals/): Google's user-experience metrics covering loading, interactivity, and visual stability, which affect both rankings and conversion.
- [Programmatic SEO](https://syvora.com/glossary/programmatic-seo/): Generating many high-quality pages from structured data to capture long-tail search demand, such as services by technology or comparisons.
- [SLO (Service Level Objective)](https://syvora.com/glossary/slo/): A target for service reliability, such as 99.9 percent of requests under 300ms, that turns uptime from a feeling into a budget.
- [Canary Deployment](https://syvora.com/glossary/canary-deployment/): Releasing a change to a small slice of traffic first, watching real metrics, then rolling forward or back.
- [GitOps](https://syvora.com/glossary/gitops/): Operating infrastructure and deployments through pull requests, with a controller reconciling the cluster to what the repository declares.
- [Headless Commerce](https://syvora.com/glossary/headless-commerce/): Separating the storefront frontend from the commerce backend, trading platform defaults for full experience control.
